title: ptelea mollis
intro: Ptelea mollis, also known as the hoptree, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain a compound called limonin, a triterpenoid with potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ptelea mollis include its ability to aid digestion, reduce fever, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Ptelea mollis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
